task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application is open and the title bar, xsheet, or scene browser displays 'dwanko_run'.

task_completion.description: Import the 'parchment_texture.jpg' file into the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The xsheet, timeline, or level strip shows 'parchment_texture' loaded as a column or layer.

task_completion.description: Ensure the texture covers the entire 1920x1080 camera frame.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewer shows the parchment texture completely filling the rectangular camera boundary without any empty gaps at the edges.

task_completion.description: Composite the texture over the animation using a 'Multiply' blending mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FX schematic or column blending settings show 'Multiply' applied to the texture layer, and the viewer visually confirms the character is visible through the darkened texture.

task_completion.description: Configure render settings to output PNG files for frames 1 to 10.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog shows the file format set to 'png' (or similar image sequence format) and the frame range explicitly set from 1 to 10.
The Output Settings dialog shows the save path as '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/textured_composite/' and a render progress bar or file manager confirms the files were generated.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to perform the compositing and rendering.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with OpenToonz menus, xsheet, schematic, and render dialogs, rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick or ffmpeg.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing the .tnz XML file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz graphical interface is used for all modifications, with no text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) open modifying the scene file.

integrity.description: The output files were generated by OpenToonz's render engine.
integrity.visual_evidence: A render progress window is visible in OpenToonz, or the output directory is shown being populated immediately after clicking render in the GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Scene path: /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz. Texture path: /home/ga/OpenToonz/inputs/parchment_texture.jpg. Output directory: /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/textured_composite. Minimum frames required: 10. Expected resolution: 1920x1080.
